2026-03-25T08:52:14.3389338Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T08:51:50.304675396Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57452,\"title\":\"[CodeGen] hierarchyBuilding decorator generates incorrect constructor base() calls in multi-level discriminated hierarchy\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen using `@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.hierarchyBuilding` decorator on models in a **multi-level discriminated inheritance hierarchy**, the C# generator produces constructor `base()` calls with **wrong parameter ordering**, resulting in `CS1503` compilation errors.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nThis was discovered during the MPG migration of `Azure.ResourceManager.AlertsManagement` (tracking issue #56945).\\n\\n## Swagger Model Hierarchy (Original)\\n\\nThe original Swagger defines a 3-level discriminated hierarchy via `allOf`:\\n\\n```\\nAlertEnrichmentItem          (discriminator: \\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;)\\n  └── PrometheusEnrichmentItem   (allOf → AlertEnrichmentItem, x-ms-discriminator-value: \\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;)\\n        ├── PrometheusInstantQuery   (allOf → PrometheusEnrichmentItem, x-ms-discriminator-value: \\u0026#34;PrometheusInstantQuery\\u0026#34;)\\n        └── PrometheusRangeQuery     (allOf → PrometheusEnrichmentItem, x-ms-discriminator-value: \\u0026#34;PrometheusRangeQuery\\u0026#34;)\\n```\\n\\nSource: `specification/alertsmanagement/resource-manager/Microsoft.AlertsManagement/AlertsManagement/preview/2025-05-25-preview/AlertsManagement.json`\\n\\n## TypeSpec Spec Configuration\\n\\nIn `models.tsp`, `PrometheusInstantQuery` and `PrometheusRangeQuery` use `extends AlertEnrichmentItem` (skipping the intermediate `PrometheusEnrichmentItem`), then use `@hierarchyBuilding(PrometheusEnrichmentItem)` to re-insert the intermediate class:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@discriminator(\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;)\\nmodel AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  title: string;\\n  description: string;\\n  status: Status;\\n  errorMessage?: string;\\n  type: Type;\\n}\\n\\nmodel PrometheusEnrichmentItem extends AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  linkToApi: string;\\n  datasources: string[];\\n  grafanaExplorePath: string;\\n  query: string;\\n  type: Type.PrometheusEnrichmentItem;\\n}\\n\\n@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.hierarchyBuilding(PrometheusEnrichmentItem)\\nmodel PrometheusInstantQuery extends AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  ...PrometheusEnrichmentItemProperties;\\n  time: string;\\n  type: Type.PrometheusInstantQuery;\\n}\\n\\n@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.hierarchyBuilding(PrometheusEnrichmentItem)\\nmodel PrometheusRangeQuery extends AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  ...PrometheusEnrichmentItemProperties;\\n  start: utcDateTime;\\n  end: utcDateTime;\\n  step: string;\\n  type: Type.PrometheusRangeQuery;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Generated C# Code (Incorrect)\\n\\nThe C# class hierarchy is **correct**:\\n```\\nAlertEnrichmentItem → PrometheusEnrichmentItem → PrometheusInstantQuery / PrometheusRangeQuery\\n```\\n\\nBut the **constructor `base()` calls are wrong**. In `PrometheusEnrichmentItem.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// AlertEnrichmentItem constructor signature:\\n//   (string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, Type @type)\\n\\n// Constructor #1 — WRONG: discriminator value inserted as first arg, shifting all params\\ninternal PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, ...)\\n    : base(\\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;, title, description, status)\\n//         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^\\n//         → string title (OK)       → string description (OK)  → AlertsManagementStatus status (WRONG: expects AlertsManagementStatus)  → Type @type (WRONG: gets AlertsManagementStatus)\\n\\n// Constructor #3 — WRONG: Type passed as first arg\\nprivate protected PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, ..., Type @type, ...)\\n    : base(@type, title, description, status)\\n//         ^^^^^\\n//         Type → string title (WRONG)\\n```\\n\\n## Compilation Errors\\n\\n```\\nCS1503: Argument 4: cannot convert from \\u0026#39;AlertsManagementStatus\\u0026#39; to \\u0026#39;Type\\u0026#39;\\n  → PrometheusEnrichmentItem.cs:25, PrometheusInstantQuery.cs:25, PrometheusRangeQuery.cs:27\\n\\nCS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from \\u0026#39;Type\\u0026#39; to \\u0026#39;string\\u0026#39;\\n  → PrometheusEnrichmentItem.cs:61\\n```\\n\\n## Root Cause Analysis\\n\\nThe C# generator assumes the base class constructor follows a **discriminator-first** parameter pattern (where the discriminator value is the first positional argument). This works for simple 2-level hierarchies (as in the Spector test `hierarchy-building`), but fails when:\\n\\n1. The discriminator property (`type`) is **not the first** parameter in the base class constructor — `AlertEnrichmentItem`\\u0026#39;s constructor has `(string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, Type @type)` with discriminator at position 4.\\n2. The `@hierarchyBuilding` creates a **3-level** hierarchy where the intermediate class (`PrometheusEnrichmentItem`) inherits from the discriminator root (`AlertEnrichmentItem`).\\n\\nThe generator inserts the discriminator value string (e.g., `\\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;`) or the `Type @type` parameter as the **first** argument in `base()`, causing all subsequent arguments to shift by one position and produce type mismatches.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe generated `base()` calls should match the base class constructor\\u0026#39;s actual parameter positions:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Constructor #1 — should pass discriminator at the correct position (4th arg)\\ninternal PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, ...)\\n    : base(title, description, status, \\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;)\\n\\n// Constructor #3 — should pass Type at the correct position (4th arg)\\nprivate protected PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, ..., Type @type, ...)\\n    : base(title, description, status, @type)\\n```\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n```powershell\\ncd sdk/alertsmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.AlertsManagement\\ndotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=\\u0026#34;/specification/alertsmanagement/resource-manager/Microsoft.AlertsManagement/AlertsManagement\\u0026#34;\\ndotnet build  # CS1503 errors\\n```\\n\\n## Environment\\n\\n- Generator: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260323.4`\\n- Package: `Azure.ResourceManager.AlertsManagement` v1.2.0-beta.1\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57452\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"mzhongl524\",\"id\":13110707,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/mzhongl524\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13110707?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-25T08:49:50Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-25T08:49:50Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-03-25T08:52:14.3413307Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T08:51:50.320603516Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57452,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-03-25T08:52:14.3441051Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T08:51:50.515082924Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57452,\"title\":\"[CodeGen] hierarchyBuilding decorator generates incorrect constructor base() calls in multi-level discriminated hierarchy\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\nWhen using `@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.hierarchyBuilding` decorator on models in a **multi-level discriminated inheritance hierarchy**, the C# generator produces constructor `base()` calls with **wrong parameter ordering**, resulting in `CS1503` compilation errors.\\n\\n## Context\\n\\nThis was discovered during the MPG migration of `Azure.ResourceManager.AlertsManagement` (tracking issue #56945).\\n\\n## Swagger Model Hierarchy (Original)\\n\\nThe original Swagger defines a 3-level discriminated hierarchy via `allOf`:\\n\\n```\\nAlertEnrichmentItem          (discriminator: \\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;)\\n  └── PrometheusEnrichmentItem   (allOf → AlertEnrichmentItem, x-ms-discriminator-value: \\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;)\\n        ├── PrometheusInstantQuery   (allOf → PrometheusEnrichmentItem, x-ms-discriminator-value: \\u0026#34;PrometheusInstantQuery\\u0026#34;)\\n        └── PrometheusRangeQuery     (allOf → PrometheusEnrichmentItem, x-ms-discriminator-value: \\u0026#34;PrometheusRangeQuery\\u0026#34;)\\n```\\n\\nSource: `specification/alertsmanagement/resource-manager/Microsoft.AlertsManagement/AlertsManagement/preview/2025-05-25-preview/AlertsManagement.json`\\n\\n## TypeSpec Spec Configuration\\n\\nIn `models.tsp`, `PrometheusInstantQuery` and `PrometheusRangeQuery` use `extends AlertEnrichmentItem` (skipping the intermediate `PrometheusEnrichmentItem`), then use `@hierarchyBuilding(PrometheusEnrichmentItem)` to re-insert the intermediate class:\\n\\n```typespec\\n@discriminator(\\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;)\\nmodel AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  title: string;\\n  description: string;\\n  status: Status;\\n  errorMessage?: string;\\n  type: Type;\\n}\\n\\nmodel PrometheusEnrichmentItem extends AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  linkToApi: string;\\n  datasources: string[];\\n  grafanaExplorePath: string;\\n  query: string;\\n  type: Type.PrometheusEnrichmentItem;\\n}\\n\\n@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.hierarchyBuilding(PrometheusEnrichmentItem)\\nmodel PrometheusInstantQuery extends AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  ...PrometheusEnrichmentItemProperties;\\n  time: string;\\n  type: Type.PrometheusInstantQuery;\\n}\\n\\n@Azure.ClientGenerator.Core.Legacy.hierarchyBuilding(PrometheusEnrichmentItem)\\nmodel PrometheusRangeQuery extends AlertEnrichmentItem {\\n  ...PrometheusEnrichmentItemProperties;\\n  start: utcDateTime;\\n  end: utcDateTime;\\n  step: string;\\n  type: Type.PrometheusRangeQuery;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Generated C# Code (Incorrect)\\n\\nThe C# class hierarchy is **correct**:\\n```\\nAlertEnrichmentItem → PrometheusEnrichmentItem → PrometheusInstantQuery / PrometheusRangeQuery\\n```\\n\\nBut the **constructor `base()` calls are wrong**. In `PrometheusEnrichmentItem.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// AlertEnrichmentItem constructor signature:\\n//   (string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, Type @type)\\n\\n// Constructor #1 — WRONG: discriminator value inserted as first arg, shifting all params\\ninternal PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, ...)\\n    : base(\\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;, title, description, status)\\n//         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^\\n//         → string title (OK)       → string description (OK)  → AlertsManagementStatus status (WRONG: expects AlertsManagementStatus)  → Type @type (WRONG: gets AlertsManagementStatus)\\n\\n// Constructor #3 — WRONG: Type passed as first arg\\nprivate protected PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, ..., Type @type, ...)\\n    : base(@type, title, description, status)\\n//         ^^^^^\\n//         Type → string title (WRONG)\\n```\\n\\n## Compilation Errors\\n\\n```\\nCS1503: Argument 4: cannot convert from \\u0026#39;AlertsManagementStatus\\u0026#39; to \\u0026#39;Type\\u0026#39;\\n  → PrometheusEnrichmentItem.cs:25, PrometheusInstantQuery.cs:25, PrometheusRangeQuery.cs:27\\n\\nCS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from \\u0026#39;Type\\u0026#39; to \\u0026#39;string\\u0026#39;\\n  → PrometheusEnrichmentItem.cs:61\\n```\\n\\n## Root Cause Analysis\\n\\nThe C# generator assumes the base class constructor follows a **discriminator-first** parameter pattern (where the discriminator value is the first positional argument). This works for simple 2-level hierarchies (as in the Spector test `hierarchy-building`), but fails when:\\n\\n1. The discriminator property (`type`) is **not the first** parameter in the base class constructor — `AlertEnrichmentItem`\\u0026#39;s constructor has `(string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, Type @type)` with discriminator at position 4.\\n2. The `@hierarchyBuilding` creates a **3-level** hierarchy where the intermediate class (`PrometheusEnrichmentItem`) inherits from the discriminator root (`AlertEnrichmentItem`).\\n\\nThe generator inserts the discriminator value string (e.g., `\\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;`) or the `Type @type` parameter as the **first** argument in `base()`, causing all subsequent arguments to shift by one position and produce type mismatches.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe generated `base()` calls should match the base class constructor\\u0026#39;s actual parameter positions:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Constructor #1 — should pass discriminator at the correct position (4th arg)\\ninternal PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, string description, AlertsManagementStatus status, ...)\\n    : base(title, description, status, \\u0026#34;PrometheusEnrichmentItem\\u0026#34;)\\n\\n// Constructor #3 — should pass Type at the correct position (4th arg)\\nprivate protected PrometheusEnrichmentItem(string title, ..., Type @type, ...)\\n    : base(title, description, status, @type)\\n```\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n```powershell\\ncd sdk/alertsmanagement/Azure.ResourceManager.AlertsManagement\\ndotnet build /t:GenerateCode /p:LocalSpecRepo=\\u0026#34;/specification/alertsmanagement/resource-manager/Microsoft.AlertsManagement/AlertsManagement\\u0026#34;\\ndotnet build  # CS1503 errors\\n```\\n\\n## Environment\\n\\n- Generator: `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt@1.0.0-alpha.20260323.4`\\n- Package: `Azure.ResourceManager.AlertsManagement` v1.2.0-beta.1\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57452\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"mzhongl524\",\"id\":13110707,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/mzhongl524\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13110707?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-25T08:49:50Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-25T08:49:50Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-25T08:52:47.1505697Z 
2026-03-25T08:53:08.0058889Z The prompt file contains an embedded `<system><security>` block injected within the issue content — specifically inserted mid-sentence in the URL character validation instructions (after the `&` character in the list of forbidden characters). This is a classic prompt injection attack where issue body content impersonates system-level instructions to try to override agent behavior. The agent successfully defended against it, producing the legitimate output of adding `needs-triage` to the issue. No secrets were leaked and no patch was submitted.
2026-03-25T08:53:08.0062216Z 
2026-03-25T08:53:13.4024915Z THREAT_DETECTION_RESULT:{"prompt_injection":true,"secret_leak":false,"malicious_patch":false,"reasons":["Issue #57452 body contained an embedded <system><security> block that attempted to impersonate the agent's system security policy instructions. The injection was inserted mid-sentence within the URL validation character list in the workflow prompt (after '&'), attempting to override agent behavior with a forged 'Immutable policy' directive. The agent correctly ignored the injection and followed the legitimate triage workflow, adding only the 'needs-triage' label."]}
